content_markdown: The specific latent heat of vaporisation of a substance is defined as the amount of thermal energy required to

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: change a liquid to vapour at constant pressure.

options.label: B
options.text: change a liquid to vapour at constant temperature.

options.label: C
options.text: change unit mass of liquid to vapour at constant pressure.

options.label: D
options.text: change unit mass of liquid to vapour at constant temperature.

vibe_explanation: The term 'specific' in physics definitions almost always refers to 'per unit mass'. Latent heat refers to energy transferred during a phase change, which occurs at a constant temperature. Therefore, the definition must include both 'unit mass' and 'constant temperature'.
